description: A line that marks the limits of an area; a dividing line. 'Norui' names the limit that defines—the border that creates identity by marking what is inside from what is outside.
sound_symbolism: The word defines with quiet firmness. 'No' begins with grounded presence, the boundary simply being there. 'Rui' extends the line with gentle repetition. A boundary that holds not by force but by clear, present definition.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun; the possessor precedes it: 'kosha norui' (the field's boundary). Related to 'shuna' (edge) but emphasizes the concept of limit rather than the physical line.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Boundaries are conventions—useful for navigation but ultimately empty of inherent existence.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Healthy boundaries enable relationship—knowing where I end and you begin allows true meeting.
